summary: Iteration 5 backend testing: CV upload now triggers Claude Sonnet 4.5 analysis to pre-fill first_name/last_name/core_skills/key_experiences. Verified (1) POST /api/employee/cv response includes a well-shaped `analysis` dict, (2) the actual LLM call succeeds end-to-end (Claude Sonnet 4.5 returned Anna/Muster + real skills + experience summary from an fpdf2-generated CV), (3) blank PDFs gracefully return `_warning='no_text'` + empty strings, (4) POST /api/employee/cv/analyze returns 404 without a CV, 403 for employer, 401 unauthenticated, and 200 + analysis dict with an existing CV, (5) the CV bytes are NOT modified by analysis (round-trip download equals upload). 10/10 new iter5 tests pass; full regression 102/102 (iter1+2+3+4) also passes for a total of 112/112.
action_items: Carry-over from iter3/4 (still applicable): server.py is now ~956 lines including CV + reanalyze routes; refactor into routers/auth.py, routers/employee.py, routers/employer.py, routers/admin.py to respect the <700 lines guideline.

cv_analyzer.analyze_cv reads the WHOLE PDF synchronously via pypdf inside the FastAPI async handler. For large multi-page CVs this blocks the event loop – consider asyncio.to_thread for _extract_pdf_text.

Server logs `logger.exception` on analysis failure (good) but the response body silently swallows the exception type. Consider also stamping the warning with a short reason (e.g. _warning_detail='budget_exceeded') to ease debugging via the API.
critical_code_review_comments: cv_analyzer.py SYSTEM_PROMPT is hard-coded German – fine for a CH portal but should at least be documented as i18n-locked.
_extract_json uses a greedy `\{.*\}` regex – if Claude prefixes the answer with stray braces, this can still parse incorrectly; consider json.loads-with-decoder fallback.
analyze_cv catches every Exception under one '_warning=analysis_failed' bucket; differentiating between LLM HTTP errors, JSON parse errors and timeouts would help observability.

POST /api/employee/cv now awaits the LLM call inside the request – this adds 2-10s latency to upload. Consider returning the response immediately and triggering analysis as a background task that the client polls via /cv/analyze.
cv_analyzer reads up to MAX_TEXT_CHARS=25_000 chars but feeds them in a single user message – for very long CVs the LLM may still truncate. Acceptable for an MVP.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@jobportal.ch / Admin123! (auto-seeded). Per-run test users test_it5_*_<SFX>@example.com / Password123! created and deleted automatically.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 3 module-scoped users (1 employee with real CV, 1 employee with blank PDF, 1 employer) + 1 fresh employee per `test_reanalyze_without_cv_returns_404`. Real Emergent object storage stores 2 small PDFs (~600B fpdf-generated, ~300B blank). All cleaned up by TestZZCleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/iteration5_test.py -v` (~10s) or full regression `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` (~110s, 112 tests). The Emergent LLM key is currently FUNCTIONAL – analyze_cv against the fpdf2-built CV actually returned `{first_name:'Anna', last_name:'Muster', core_skills:'Python, FastAPI, Projektleitung', key_experiences:'Von 2019 bis 2024 als Backend Engineer bei der Beispiel AG tätig...'}`. The tests do NOT assert on specific field values (only on shape) so they remain robust if budget is later exhausted (graceful degradation to `_warning='analysis_failed'` is also accepted). Real Emergent object storage is hit – keep test PDFs tiny.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. All iter5 acceptance criteria pass: (1) POST /api/employee/cv adds an `analysis` dict to its response with first_name/last_name/core_skills/key_experiences keys (and optional _warning), (2) blank PDF -> _warning='no_text' with empty strings, (3) POST /api/employee/cv/analyze returns 404 (no CV) / 403 (employer) / 401 (no auth) / 200+analysis (employee with CV), (4) CV bytes are not modified by analysis (round-trip equality verified), (5) all iter1+2+3+4 regression tests still pass.
